title_en: Zakat and Fasting Disputes in Al-Insaf
content_en:
Al-Mardawi's treatment of zakat and fasting disputes in Al-Insaf demonstrates the depth of internal scholarly discussion within the Hanbali school on these pillars of Islam. Questions that might seem settled in other schools often reveal multiple transmitted opinions from Imam Ahmad himself, generating ongoing scholarly debates within the Hanbali tradition.

On whether zakat is obligatory on children and the insane, the Hanbali school has two major positions. The dominant (rajih) view — which al-Mardawi affirms — holds that zakat is obligatory on their wealth, with the guardian responsible for paying it. This is based on the general nature of the zakat obligation, which applies to the wealth rather than the person. The second Hanbali view holds that zakat requires sound legal capacity (taklif), which excludes children and the insane.

The question of zakat on debts generates complex discussion. If a person is owed a debt that is likely to be recovered, is zakat due on that debt during the years it is outstanding? The Hanbali school's positions range from: pay zakat each year even on outstanding debts (the strict position), to pay all accumulated years' zakat when the debt is recovered (the moderate position), to no zakat on debts that are uncertain of recovery (the lenient position). Al-Mardawi evaluates the evidential basis for each and identifies the rajih.

For Ramadan fasting, a disputed question concerns the person who breaks their fast believing it to be nighttime, then discovers the sun had not yet set. The Hanbali school is more lenient than some other schools in this scenario — one transmitted position holds the fast is valid if the person was genuinely mistaken and acted in good faith. Al-Mardawi presents the evidential debate and evaluates whether the fast must be made up or is valid.

The question of whether a traveler who begins a fast at home, then starts a journey mid-day, may break their fast that day generates multiple Hanbali views. The rajih position identified by al-Mardawi is that once the fast has been validly started, it must be completed unless there is genuine hardship from continuing — the permission to break is linked to necessity, not to the mere commencement of travel.

Zakat on honey is a distinctive area of Hanbali law: Imam Ahmad — unlike the other major imams — held that zakat is due on honey (one-tenth), based on certain hadiths that other scholars considered weak. Al-Mardawi presents the evidential arguments and the internal Hanbali discussion, noting that the position that zakat is due on honey is the dominant Hanbali view even though it is not shared by the other schools.

For the kaffarah of intentionally breaking the Ramadan fast, the Hanbali school (following the Maliki and Shafi'i schools) holds that the kaffarah applies specifically to breaking the fast through sexual intercourse — not to breaking it through eating or drinking. Al-Mardawi presents this position as the rajih against the Hanafi school's application of kaffarah to all intentional breaks, with careful attention to the evidential basis of both positions.
title_id: Perselisihan Zakat dan Puasa dalam Al-Inshaf
content_id:
Perlakuan al-Mardawi terhadap perselisihan zakat dan puasa dalam Al-Inshaf mendemonstrasikan kedalaman diskusi ilmiah internal dalam madzhab Hanbali tentang rukun-rukun Islam ini. Pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang mungkin tampak sudah ditetapkan dalam madzhab lain sering kali mengungkapkan beberapa pendapat yang diriwayatkan dari Imam Ahmad sendiri, menghasilkan perdebatan ulama yang berkelanjutan dalam tradisi Hanbali.

Tentang apakah zakat wajib atas anak-anak dan orang gila, madzhab Hanbali memiliki dua posisi utama. Pandangan dominan (rajih) — yang ditegaskan oleh al-Mardawi — menyatakan bahwa zakat wajib atas harta mereka, dengan wali yang bertanggung jawab membayarnya. Ini didasarkan pada sifat umum kewajiban zakat, yang berlaku pada harta bukan pada orangnya. Pandangan Hanbali kedua menyatakan bahwa zakat memerlukan kapasitas hukum yang sehat (taklif), yang mengecualikan anak-anak dan orang gila.

Pertanyaan tentang zakat atas piutang menghasilkan diskusi yang kompleks. Jika seseorang memiliki piutang yang kemungkinan besar akan ditagih, apakah zakat harus dibayarkan atas piutang tersebut selama tahun-tahun ia belum terlunasi? Posisi-posisi madzhab Hanbali berkisar dari: bayar zakat setiap tahun bahkan atas piutang yang belum terlunasi (posisi ketat), hingga bayar semua zakat tahun-tahun yang terakumulasi ketika piutang dilunasi (posisi moderat), hingga tidak ada zakat atas piutang yang tidak pasti penagihannya (posisi longgar). Al-Mardawi mengevaluasi dasar evidensial untuk masing-masing dan mengidentifikasi yang rajih.

Untuk puasa Ramadhan, pertanyaan yang diperdebatkan menyangkut orang yang membatalkan puasanya karena mengira sudah malam, kemudian mendapati bahwa matahari belum terbenam. Madzhab Hanbali lebih longgar dibandingkan beberapa madzhab lain dalam skenario ini — satu pendapat yang diteruskan menyatakan bahwa puasa tetap sah jika orang tersebut sungguh-sungguh keliru dan bertindak dengan itikad baik. Al-Mardawi menyajikan perdebatan evidensial dan mengevaluasi apakah puasa harus diqadha atau sah adanya.

Pertanyaan apakah musafir yang memulai puasa di rumah, kemudian memulai perjalanan di tengah hari, boleh membatalkan puasanya hari itu menghasilkan beberapa pandangan Hanbali. Posisi rajih yang diidentifikasi al-Mardawi adalah bahwa sekali puasa dimulai secara sah, puasa itu harus diselesaikan kecuali ada kesulitan nyata dalam melanjutkannya — izin untuk membatalkan dikaitkan dengan kebutuhan, bukan dengan sekadar dimulainya perjalanan.

Zakat atas madu adalah bidang khas hukum Hanbali: Imam Ahmad — tidak seperti para imam utama lainnya — berpendapat bahwa zakat wajib atas madu (sepersepuluh), berdasarkan hadis-hadis tertentu yang dianggap lemah oleh ulama lain. Al-Mardawi menyajikan posisi-posisi Hanbali tentang masalah ini dan mengevaluasi kekuatan relatif bukti, menggambarkan bagaimana madzhab Hanbali mempertahankan komitmennya pada hadis bahkan ketika itu membawanya ke posisi yang tidak dianut oleh madzhab-madzhab lain.

Dalam perlakuannya terhadap perselisihan zakat dan puasa, Al-Inshaf berfungsi sebagai sumber informasi yang tak ternilai tentang keluasan dan kedalaman diskusi hukum dalam madzhab Hanbali. Karya ini menunjukkan bahwa bahkan dalam domain-domain yang tampaknya sudah mapan dari praktik keagamaan Islam, terdapat ruang yang substansial untuk perbedaan ilmiah yang sah, dan bahwa madzhab Hanbali — seperti tradisi hukum besar lainnya — adalah badan pemikiran hukum yang hidup dan berkembang, bukan sekadar daftar aturan yang ditetapkan.
content_ar: Al-Mardawi's treatment of الزكاة and الصيام disputes in Al-Insaf demonstrates the depth of internal العلمي discussion within the الحنبلي school on these pillars of الإسلام. Questions that might seem settled in other schools often reveal multiple transmitted opinions from الإمام أحمد himself, generating ongoing العلمي debates within the الحنبلي التراث. On whether الزكاة is obligatory on الأولاد and the insane, the الحنبلي school has two major positions. The dominant (rajih) view — which al-Mardawi affirms — holds that الزكاة is obligatory on their الثروة, with the guardian responsible for paying it. This is based on the general nature of the الزكاة الالتزام, which applies to the الثروة rather than the person. The ثانياً الحنبلي view holds that الزكاة requires sound legal capacity (taklif), which excludes الأولاد and the insane. The question of الزكاة on debts generates complex discussion. إذا a person is owed a debt that is likely to be recovered, is الزكاة due on that debt خلال the years it is outstanding? The الحنبلي school's positions range from: pay الزكاة each year even on outstanding debts (the strict position), to pay all accumulated years' الزكاة عندما the debt is recovered (the moderate position), to no الزكاة on debts that are uncertain of recovery (the lenient position). Al-Mardawi evaluates the evidential basis for each and identifies the rajih. For Ramadan الصيام, a disputed question concerns the person who breaks their fast believing it to be nighttime, then discovers the sun had not yet set. The الحنبلي school is more lenient than some other schools in this scenario — one transmitted position holds the fast is valid إذا the person was genuinely mistaken and acted in الخير الإيمان. Al-Mardawi presents the evidential debate and evaluates whether the fast must be made up or is valid. The question of whether a traveler who begins a fast at home, then starts a journey mid-day, may break their fast that day generates multiple الحنبلي views. The rajih position identified by al-Mardawi is that حين the fast has been validly started, it must be completed إلا إذا there is genuine الشدة from continuing — the permission to break is linked to necessity, not to the mere commencement of travel. الزكاة on honey is a distinctive area of الحنبلي law: الإمام أحمد — unlike the other major imams — held that الزكاة is due on honey (one-عاشراً), based on certain hadiths that other العلماء considered weak. Al-Mardawi presents the evidential arguments and the internal الحنبلي discussion, noting that the position that الزكاة is due on honey is the dominant الحنبلي view حتى وإن it is not shared by the other schools. For the kaffarah of intentionally breaking the Ramadan fast, the الحنبلي school (following the المالكي and الشافعي schools) holds that the kaffarah applies specifically to breaking the fast through sexual intercourse — not to breaking it through eating or drinking.
